phonetic: 藞k蓴藧s蓹let; 藢k蓴藧s蓹藞let
examples: Although most of their scales have been lost, tunas and mackerels retain a patch of coarse scales near the head called the corselet.
Cutlass and corselet of steel, and his trusty sword of Damascus.
Stoniness of human makes us feel quite lonely in the noisy world, just like wearing thick corselet.
